mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity-sensitive retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0% after a sustained tightening phase, the higher discount-rate burden dominates nominal growth support and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% and is higher than 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll expansion and falling unemployment indicate broad income growth without visible labor-market stress, raising expected revenues and risk appetite, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform single-variable PAYEMS or UNRATE signals and its 60-day hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment identifies a contractionary production-labor shock that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls over 3 months and UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases are not associated with below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the coefficient on the interaction is nonnegative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in housing starts from elevated levels signals a reversal in interest-rate-sensitive durable demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average after exceeding 1800
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downward-crossing events from above 1800 are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while policy rates are elevated forces investors to price further tightening and lower equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS is at least 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction has a positive walk-forward coefficient or if triggered 20-day returns are not lower than unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while the federal funds rate is already high, strong demand becomes a tightening-risk signal rather than a growth signal, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS is at least 4.0%
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with resilient demand
failure_condition: Reject if the 5-day return response to RSAFS acceleration is positive when FEDFUNDS is at least 4.0%, or if the interaction adds no predictive power over RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate crossing above 5.0% signals that labor slack is becoming visible to equity investors, lowering expected consumption and earnings, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% or rises by more than 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crossing events are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns and a hit rate below 50% for the negative-return prediction.
